message:
linalg: add tensorsolve parity with numpy.linalg.tensorsolve

Wire tensorsolve through the four-layer parity stack (core linalg,
python wrapper, conformance runner, differential fixtures) so that a
tensor-shaped linear system reshaped down to a 2D solve and back up
preserves numpy's output shape exactly.

Core (crates/fnp-linalg/src/lib.rs):
- Export tensorsolve so upstream crates can call it without going
  through validate_backend_bridge.
- Add unit coverage that an identity 2x2x2x2x2x2 tensor solve returns
  the rhs in the reshaped [2,2,2] output shape rather than a flattened
  vector, which matches numpy's post-solve reshape contract.

Python wrapper (crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs):
- Add a tensorsolve(a, b) binding that accepts PyAny tensors, resolves
  shape/rhs_shape the same way numpy does (all but the last len(b.shape)
  axes are the matrix axes), and returns a PyAny tensor whose shape is
  a.shape[len(b.shape):]. Wire it into the fnp_python module export.
- Add a regression test that the binding matches numpy's tensor output
  shape for a non-trivial identity case so future refactors can't
  quietly flatten the result.

Conformance (crates/fnp-conformance/src/lib.rs):
- Extend execute_linalg_operation with a "tensorsolve" arm that flattens
  the matrix fixture, calls tensorsolve, and wraps the result as
  LinalgOperationOutcome::SolveVector for differential comparison.
- Extend validate_linalg_differential_expectation so the SolveVector
  arm accepts "tensorsolve" alongside the existing batch_* operations
  when checking expected_solution length/values.

Fixture (crates/fnp-conformance/fixtures/linalg_differential_cases.json):
- Add linalg_tensorsolve_identity_tensor_2x2x2 case pinned to numpy
  2.4.3: an 8x8 identity flattened from a 2x2x2x2x2x2 tensor solved
  against [0..7] rhs, expected_solution = rhs (identity), tolerance
  1e-12. This locks the shape/value contract end-to-end.

Why: closes the gap where numpy users could call tensorsolve but
franken_numpy would fall through validate_linalg_differential_expectation
without a matching arm, causing silent pass-through or shape drift.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
